column1: In sub-Saharan Africa, growth is projected to rise from an estimated 3.3 percent in 2023 to 3.8 percent in 2024 as the negative effects of
earlier weather shocks subside and supply issues gradually improve. As macro-fiscal reforms gradually bear fruits, Nigeria is expected to
post improved growth in 2024, projected at 3.3 percent. The country’s inflation is expected to gradually ease as the effects of last year's
exchange rate adjustments and elimination of fuel subsidies diminish. Also, the structural reforms are expected to bolster Nigeria's fiscal
